title: Volumes, PersistentVolumes, Claims, StorageClasses, CSI, Snapshots, and Recovery · Cluster Repair Bay
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective stateful storage and recovery contract; preserve status and events, isolate the first causal fault in volumes, persistentvolumes, claims, storageclasses, csi, snapshots, and recovery, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in stateful storage and recovery contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pra…
objectives: Distinguish image, writable layer, emptyDir, projected, hostPath, ephemeral CSI, persistent, and external application storage lifecycles and authority.
Trace PV and PVC capacity, access modes, volume mode, selectors, binding, claim references, node affinity, phases, expansion, and release.
Design StorageClasses with provisioner, parameters, defaulting, reclaim policy, expansion, mount options, and WaitForFirstConsumer topology behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug K8s App Consistent Backup Restore
steps.instruction:
Order the K8s App Consistent Backup Restore orchestration evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug K8s App Consistent Backup Restore.
steps.why: Correct order keeps K8s App Consistent Backup Restore assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug K8s App Consistent Backup Restore, keep K8s App Consistent Backup Restore connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A debug StatefulSet loses a node while its PVC is bound in another zone, reclaim policy is Delete, expansion is pending, and the backup has never restored. Defend StorageClass, binding, access mode, topology, snapshot, restore, and application consistency. Competency probe: Define application quiescence, snapshot or logical backup, metadata, encryption, checksums, retention, cross-cluster restore, and changed-case validation.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so K8s App Consistent Backup Restore has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Order the regression proof · Debug K8s App Consistent Backup Restore, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s App Consistent Backup Restore precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Order the regression proof · Debug K8s App Consistent Backup Restore, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A debug StatefulSet loses a node while its PVC is bound in another zone, reclaim policy is Delete, expansion is pending, and the backup has never restored. Defend StorageClass, binding, access mode, topology, snapshot, restore, and application consistency. Competency probe: Define application quiescence, snapshot or logical backup, metadata, encryption, checksums, retention, cross-cluster restore, and changed-case validation.

For Order the regression proof · Debug K8s App Consistent Backup Restore, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: A Bound PVC and successful VolumeSnapshot prove the application can recover within its objectives.

options.text: Capture client and server versions, context, namespace, API discovery, current object state, ownership, and immutable release identity for the debug K8s App Consistent Backup Restore case
Predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, security, disruption, and cleanup effects for the debug K8s App Consistent Backup Restore case
Review the smallest manifest, Kustomize, Helm, policy, or runbook increment in the deterministic browser simulator for the debug K8s App Consistent Backup Restore case
Reject one failure, record repair and rollback, then name authorized disposable-cluster transfer evidence for the debug K8s App Consistent Backup Restore case

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s App Consistent Backup Restore
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-app-consistent-backup-restore-16gm90". Tailor its storage-recovery.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s App Consistent Backup Restore.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s App Consistent Backup Restore into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s App Consistent Backup Restore, keep K8s App Consistent Backup Restore connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-app-consistent-backup-restore-16gm90
  - competency: k8s-app-consistent-backup-restore
    artifact: storage-recovery.yaml
    decision: app consistent backup restore
    environment: air-gapped release cluster using an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: restore a claim in a different topology and numeric identity
    retained-proof: Pod state previous exit endpoint readiness and request result
    case-key: 16gm90
    change: |
      claim: intake-data
      storageClass: retained-zonal
      volumeBindingMode: WaitForFirstConsumer
      reclaimPolicy: Retain
      snapshot: application-quiesced
      restore: fresh-namespace-and-claim
      checksum: verified
    verify: kubectl get pv,pvc,storageclass,volumesnapshot -A -o json
    failure: Reject Bound as durability proof, Delete reclaim surprises, arbitrary-zone binding, snapshot-as-backup claims, or restore without application validation.
    repair: Trace claim and volume lifecycle, align topology and access, quiesce the application, protect metadata and keys, and rehearse clean restore.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The K8s App Consistent Backup Restore structural check is only one gate. stateful storage and recovery contract must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.why: Retrieving K8s App Consistent Backup Restore through the concrete stateful storage and recovery contract case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s App Consistent Backup Restore, keep K8s App Consistent Backup Restore connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define application quiescence, snapshot or logical backup, metadata, encryption, checksums, retention, cross-cluster restore, and changed-case validation.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s App Consistent Backup Restore, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s App Consistent Backup Restore precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Publish the incident record · Debug K8s App Consistent Backup Restore, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A debug StatefulSet loses a node while its PVC is bound in another zone, reclaim policy is Delete, expansion is pending, and the backup has never restored. Defend StorageClass, binding, access mode, topology, snapshot, restore, and application consistency. Competency probe: Define application quiescence, snapshot or logical backup, metadata, encryption, checksums, retention, cross-cluster restore, and changed-case validation.

For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s App Consistent Backup Restore, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: A Bound PVC and successful VolumeSnapshot prove the application can recover within its objectives.
